subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: multilateral (IMF, World Bank, EBRD, WJP, RSF, TI, V-Dem, Freedom House, OSCE) + Georgian official (Geostat, NBG) + Georgian independent press (Civil.ge, OC Media); Russian state media excluded.

Scored governance on observable actions/data (CPI drop, RSF rank, OSCE findings, the Georgian-Dream-passed laws, the self-imposed EU-accession freeze), not opposition rhetoric; government-side strengths confirmed by neutral data (low debt, sound banks, low crime, open immigration) kept at full positive weight.
NEGATIVE-SKEW case: good fundamentals undercut by accelerating state capture, democratic backsliding, and Russia-threat exposure.

economic.notes: Good fundamentals (low debt, sound banks, strong growth) that the deteriorating governance now threatens.

fiscal_state.notes: Public debt ~36% GDP, back to pre-pandemic levels, with the deficit under the fiscal-rule ceiling; strong recent growth. (IMF 2025/2026 Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: The NBG is technically competent (inflation-targeting), but management/board purges and political pressure since 2024 erode de facto independence; trajectory worsening. (IMF; NBG; Transparency Intl)

reserve_currency_intl_monetary.notes: The lari is not a reserve currency. (definitional)

inflation.notes: ~4-5% YoY, near the 3% target, with a stable lari. (NBG)

banking_stability.notes: Well-capitalised, record profits, NPLs ~2.6%, healthy liquidity. (NBG; KPMG 2025)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Diversified (Middle Corridor transit, tourism, wine/agri, ICT, remittances), no resource curse; heavy Russia-trade dependence and sanctions-circumvention scrutiny the risk. (IMF; CEPA)

institutional.notes: Documented state capture and a sharply declining press freedom; statistics still usable.

rule_of_law.notes: Documented state capture by the Ivanishvili/Georgian Dream network, with a judiciary controlled by a judges' clan; trajectory deteriorating. (Transparency Intl; V-Dem)

statistical_integrity.notes: Geostat is usable and IMF/WB-relied-upon, with emigration/occupied-area gaps. (IMF; Geostat)

civil_service_capacity.notes: A functional administrative core, increasingly politicized/loyalty-based, with brain drain. (TI; IMF)

press_freedom.notes: RSF rank fell sharply; 600+ press attacks in a year and a FARA-style 'foreign agents' law (with prison terms) targeting independent media. (RSF; CPJ)

political_social.notes: A democratic-backsliding crisis and a crackdown on civil liberties, against a genuinely open immigration regime.

political_stability.notes: A contested Oct 2024 election (OSCE-flagged), Georgian Dream consolidation, 200+ days of protest, and an EU-accession freeze - volatile. (OSCE/ODIHR; EU Parliament; Civil.ge)

civil_liberties.notes: Protest crackdowns and mass detentions, a foreign-agents law, and an anti-LGBT law. (Freedom House; HRW)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: Genuinely open: US citizens get 365-day visa-free stay, with easy residency/business setup; discounted from higher only by the rule-of-law backdrop (durability risk if a captured state weaponizes status). (travel.state.gov)

social_cohesion.notes: Deep, hardening polarization between a pro-EU public and the government. (EU; OC Media)

demographics.notes: Ageing with sustained youth/female emigration; population ~3.7M. (Geostat; UNFPA)

geopolitical.notes: Drifting from the West toward Russia accommodation, with ~20% of territory occupied.

alliance_reliability.notes: Drifting from the EU/US toward Russia accommodation; EU candidacy frozen, the US strategic partnership suspended, and the ruling oligarch sanctioned. (EU Parliament; US State)

conflict_involvement.notes: ~20% of territory (Abkhazia/South Ossetia) occupied since 2008, with ongoing 'borderization' and a latent Russia threat. (US State Dept; CRS)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Currently an open capital account with no controls, but elevated Western scrutiny over Russia sanctions-circumvention is a real downside risk. (US State Investment Climate 2025)

physical_practical.notes: Very low street crime and improving infrastructure, against uneven healthcare and seismic/landslide exposure.

climate.notes: Mountainous and temperate, with localized flood/landslide risk. (PreventionWeb)

healthcare.notes: Privatized, cheap, and improving, but uneven in quality and coverage. (WHO-region data)

infrastructure.notes: Decent and improving (the East-West highway, transit upgrades). (IMF; EBRD)

crime_safety.notes: A genuine strength: very low street crime, top-third on the Global Peace Index. (GPI 2025)

disaster_insurance.notes: Low penetration against real seismic/landslide exposure. (PreventionWeb)

summary: Genuinely strong economic fundamentals (low debt, sound banks, strong growth, low inflation), a uniquely open immigration regime for visitors, and very low street crime - undercut by an accelerating democratic-backsliding crisis: documented state capture, a foreign-agents crackdown on independent media and protest, a frozen EU-accession path, and drift toward Russia accommodation, atop ~20% of territory under Russian occupation. The outcome distribution skews negative as governance threatens the fundamentals.
